topology: Op-Amp with Silicon Diode Soft Clipping

components.role: Inverting amplifier — gain stage whose output is clamped by feedback diodes
Positive-swing clipping diode — limits positive output excursion to ~0.6V
Negative-swing clipping diode — limits negative output excursion to ~0.6V
Feedback resistor — sets pre-clip gain with Ri; diodes progressively bypass this at signal peaks
Input resistor — sets gain and input impedance
Input coupling capacitor — blocks DC, shapes low-frequency content entering the clipping stage

dsp_model.description: Gain into tanh-based soft clipper scaled to silicon diode threshold; the feedback topology creates a smooth, gradual compression

behavioral_description: The classic overdrive clipping topology used in the Tube Screamer and hundreds of derivatives. Silicon diodes in the op-amp feedback loop create a smooth, progressive compression that begins well before the hard clip threshold. At low gain settings, the sound is warm and slightly compressed with subtle overtone enhancement. As gain increases, the clipping becomes more pronounced, producing a thick, sustaining overdrive with controlled harmonic content. The ~0.6V clip threshold keeps the output level moderate. Because the diodes are in the feedback path (soft clipping), the transition from clean to clipped is gradual and musically responsive to playing dynamics.
